data.title: Nexon's game ARC Raiders® passes 12.4 million units sold, with 960,000 concurrent players in January 2025.
data.description: In Tokyo, Nexon Co., Ltd. revealed that ARC Raiders®, a new game from Embark Studios in Stockholm, has sold over 12.4 million units. Since its launch on October 30, 2025, the game has attracted 960,000 concurrent players in January, just ten weeks after its release. Junghun Lee, the President of Nexon, expressed satisfaction with the high retention rates and continuous enthusiasm from players globally in major markets for ARC Raiders.

data.title: Urged by Trump, tech companies must bear their own power costs as US energy demand rises; Microsoft takes action first.
data.description: The President of the United States has called on technology companies to cover the costs of powering data centers as energy consumption in the country has risen significantly. Microsoft has taken the lead by implementing these changes this week.
Australian Dollar Hits 106.54 Yen, Surpassing July 2024 Levels

data.title: AISpeech unveils smart mobility solutions at CES 2026, emphasizing conversational AI in the sector.
data.description: In Las Vegas on January 12, 2026, during CES 2026, AISpeech showcased its innovations in intelligent mobility. The company, a prominent conversational AI platform provider, demonstrated its latest advancements in the sector. Specifically emphasizing end-to-end conversational AI technology, AISpeech impressed attendees with its developments at the event organized by Shuzhi Society.

data.title: Senate bill seeks to clarify crypto developer liability under federal law as enforcement actions rise, aiming to address when and how they can be held accountable.
data.description: A proposed bill seeks to establish guidelines for determining the circumstances under which cryptocurrency developers may be held legally responsible. This initiative comes in response to the growing number of enforcement actions and increasing public discussion on the matter.

data.title: Senator John Boozman postpones crypto bill markup to January end for broad support, cites need for bipartisan consensus.
data.description: The Senate Agriculture Committee, led by Chairman John Boozman (R-AR), has decided to delay the markup of the cryptocurrency market structure bill from Thursday to the final week of January. The postponement is aimed at allowing more time for bipartisan consensus to be reached. Boozman mentioned that the committee had productive bipartisan discussions over the weekend and had made significant progress. He emphasized the importance of finalizing the remaining details to ensure broad support for the legislation. The decision to postpone the markup was made to refine the bill further before proceeding.
